ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Text-based PDF, clear and fully readable. ICC Certificate of Origin with EU header, issued by West Sweden Chamber of Commerce.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Text-based PDF, clear and fully readable. Commercial invoice issued by J.UNGERTH AB.

lc_check.lc_verification_note: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The certificate number 'SEG-26D-465117' does not contain an LC reference in a banking context. No field labeled 'L/C', 'Letter of Credit', 'Documentary Credit' or similar is present.

locations_found: Invoice header/issuer block: 'J.UNGERTH AB' at top of page 1
Invoice footer/signature block: 'J.UNGERTH AB' at bottom of page 1
VAT block: 'SE556146063401' with SE prefix confirming Sweden
consignor_search_result.search_note: Certificate consignor 'J. UNGERTH AB' identified as 'J.UNGERTH AB' in invoice header, footer, and VAT block. The only difference is a space after 'J.' which is a minor formatting variation.

criteria_met: Consignor 'J. UNGERTH AB' identified in invoice as 'J.UNGERTH AB' (issuer/header)
Consignee 'MINA PACK' identified in invoice as buyer 'MINA PACK'
Order number '25090' in certificate goods description matches invoice Order No '25090'
pairing_validation.note: At least 3 of 3 pairing criteria are met. Document pair is valid.

candidates.raw_text: Behind El Tawheed & El Nour Plot 11, Block 12010 1st Industrial Zone, Northern Extension OBOUR CITY, EGYPT VAT 274401436
candidates.normalized_text: behind el tawheed & el nour plot 11, block 12010 1st industrial zone, northern extension obour city, egypt

candidates.raw_text: Behind El Tawheed & El Nour Plot 11, Block 12010 1st Industrial Zone, Northern Extension OBOUR CITY, EGYPT VAT 274401436
candidates.normalized_text: behind el tawheed & el nour plot 11, block 12010 1st industrial zone, northern extension obour city, egypt

checkpoints.certificate_value: J. UNGERTH AB, LILLA BOMMEN 2, SE-411 04, GÖTEBORG, SWEDEN
checkpoints.invoice_value: J.UNGERTH AB, Lilla Bommen 2, SE-411 04 Gothenburg, SWEDEN, VAT: SE556146063401

comparison_steps.description: Search for certificate consignor name 'J. UNGERTH AB' anywhere in invoice.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate consignor 'J. UNGERTH AB' identified as 'J.UNGERTH AB' in invoice header (issuer), invoice footer/signature block, and VAT block. The difference is solely a space between 'J.' and 'UNGERTH' which is a minor formatting/spacing variation allowed under normalization (extra mellanslag). The core name 'J UNGERTH AB' is identical after normalization.

Certificate states consignor country SWEDEN. Invoice issuer address explicitly states 'SWEDEN'. Additionally, VAT number SE556146063401 has SE prefix confirming Sweden. IBAN SE51... also confirms Sweden.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur – Consignor name found directly in invoice issuer block.
Country verified via explicit address and VAT prefix SE.
Central normalization – extra space between 'J.' and 'UNGERTH' normalized.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor: 'J. UNGERTH AB' at 'LILLA BOMMEN 2, SE-411 04, GÖTEBORG, SWEDEN'. Invoice issuer: 'J.UNGERTH AB' at 'Lilla Bommen 2, SE-411 04 Gothenburg, SWEDEN'. After normalization of spacing (removing extra space between 'J.' and 'UNGERTH'), the company names are identical. 'GÖTEBORG' vs 'Gothenburg' is an address difference (Swedish vs English spelling of same city) which per rule 4.1.5 does not in itself cause MISMATCH — only company name and country are decisive. Country is SWEDEN in both. MATCH per 4.1.0 and 4.1.4.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The space difference in 'J. UNGERTH' vs 'J.UNGERTH' constitutes a name mismatch.
rejected_hypotheses.reason: Rejected. Difference is solely extra whitespace, which is explicitly allowed under normalization rules (section 4, 'extra mellanslag').

checkpoints.certificate_value: MINA PACK, Behind El Tawheed & El Nour Plot 11, Block 12010 1st Industrial Zone, Northern Extension OBOUR CITY, EGYPT VAT 274401436
checkpoints.invoice_value: MINA PACK, Behind El Tawheed & El Nour Plot 11, Block 12010 1st Industrial Zone, Northern Extension OBOUR CITY, EGYPT VAT 274401436

comparison_steps.description: Invoice has 'Buyer:' field with MINA PACK.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate consignee 'MINA PACK' is identified in the invoice's primary billing field 'Buyer: MINA PACK'. This is the authoritative consignee per priority order.
'MINA PACK' in certificate exactly matches 'MINA PACK' in invoice Buyer field.
Certificate states consignee country EGYPT. Invoice buyer address includes 'EGYPT'. Country matches.

rules_applied.description: Priority 1 – Buyer field identified as primary billing field.
Company name 'MINA PACK' exactly matches in invoice.
Country EGYPT matches in both documents.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee: 'MINA PACK' with address 'Behind El Tawheed & El Nour Plot 11, Block 12010 1st Industrial Zone, Northern Extension OBOUR CITY, EGYPT VAT 274401436'. Invoice buyer: 'MINA PACK' with address 'Behind El Tawheed & El Nour Plot 11, Block 12010 1st Industrial Zone, Northern Extension OBOUR CITY, EGYPT VAT 274401436'. Company name is exactly identical. Address is exactly identical. Country EGYPT matches. MATCH per 4.2.4.

checkpoints.invoice_value: KRAFTLINER, Shipment in 3 container, 59 reels, Shipping Mark: ACID NUMBER: 2744014361001210016, Order No: 25090

comparison_steps.description: Check if article numbers are present in certificate.
comparison_steps.details: No explicit article numbers are stated in the certificate's goods description. '25090' is an order number, not an article number. ACID NUMBER is a shipping mark, not an article number. Proceeding to Priority 2.

comparison_steps.description: Identify the identity-bearing main designation in the certificate and search for it in the invoice.
comparison_steps.details: The identity-bearing main designation in the certificate is 'KRAFTLINER'. This exact term is found in the invoice in multiple places: (1) 'Description of Goods: KRAFTLINER', (2) 'Quality/Grade: KRAFTLINER'. Exact match after case normalization.

comparison_steps.description: Verify that additional elements in certificate do not change product identity.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate contains additional descriptors: '25090' (order number), 'EGYPT' (destination), 'ACID NUMBER: 2744014361001210016' (shipping mark), '59 reels' (packaging). All of these are non-identity-bearing additions. Invoice also contains these: Order No 25090, Shipping Mark ACID NUMBER 2744014361001210016, 59 reels. The core product 'KRAFTLINER' is identically stated.

rules_applied.description: Priority 2 – Controlled text matching. Identity-bearing main designation 'KRAFTLINER' found exactly in invoice.
Non-identity-bearing additions (order no, shipping mark, reels count) do not affect product identity.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate goods description (box 6): '25090 EGYPT ACID NUMBER: 2744014361001210016 KRAFTLINER 59 reels'. The identity-bearing main designation is 'KRAFTLINER'. Invoice goods: 'Description of Goods: KRAFTLINER' and 'Quality/Grade: KRAFTLINER'. Exact match. Additional elements (order number 25090, shipping mark ACID NUMBER 2744014361001210016, 59 reels) all confirmed in invoice as well but are non-identity-bearing per 4.3.4. MATCH per 4.3.3.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: '25090' could be an article number requiring exact verification.
rejected_hypotheses.reason: Rejected. '25090' is identified as the order number in the invoice ('Order No: 25090') and in the certificate it appears alongside 'EGYPT' which is a destination marker. It is not an article/product number.

comparison_steps.description: Verify that certificate states both numeric value and unit.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate box 7 states '77,102mt net'. Numeric value: 77.102. Unit: MT (metric ton). Weight category: Net. All required elements present.

comparison_steps.description: Identify the authoritative quantity from box 7.
comparison_steps.details: Box 7 contains '77,102mt net'. This is the verification-grounding quantity. Box 6 also mentions '59 reels' but box 7 takes precedence per 4.4.2.1.

Certificate: 77.102 MT net. Invoice: Quantity (MT): 77,102 with column header 'Quantity (MT)'. The numeric value 77.102 is identical. The comma in '77,102' in the invoice is a decimal separator (consistent with European notation and the column header 'Quantity (MT)' which indicates this is a metric ton value, not 77,102 pieces). Invoice value 77.102 MT matches certificate value 77.102 MT exactly.

Certificate explicitly states 'net'. Invoice column header is 'Quantity (MT)' without explicit GW/NW designation. However, per 4.4.2.2 point 2: certificate states ONE weight value without competing alternative, and this value matches the invoice exactly. The invoice does not contain a contradicting alternative weight value of another category. Per 4.4.2.2 point 2: MATCH.

rules_applied.description: Main rule – occurrence check. Numeric value 77.102 found in invoice.
Box 7 value '77,102mt net' is the verification-grounding quantity.
Certificate states one weight value (net), invoice confirms same numeric value. No contradicting alternative weight in invoice.
Multiple quantity data: box 7 (77.102 mt net) verified as primary. Box 6 (59 reels) is secondary and also confirmed in invoice (Reels: 59) but not required.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7: '77,102mt net'. Interpreted as 77.102 metric tons net weight (comma is decimal separator in European notation). Invoice: 'Quantity (MT): 77,102' in the product table. Same numeric value 77.102 in MT. The comma in the invoice value is also a decimal separator per column header 'Quantity (MT)'. Values are identical: 77.102 MT. Certificate also mentions '59 reels' in box 6, invoice confirms 'Reels: 59'. Per 4.4.3.1, at least one quantity verified (primary box 7 quantity matches). No contradicting quantities found. MATCH per 4.4.1 and 4.4.2.2 point 2.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The '77,102' in the invoice could be interpreted as 77,102 (seventy-seven thousand one hundred and two) pieces with comma as thousands separator.
rejected_hypotheses.reason: Rejected. The column header explicitly states 'Quantity (MT)' indicating metric tons, not pieces. The value 77,102 MT is consistent with a paper shipment (59 reels of kraftliner). 77,102 pieces would be inconsistent with '59 reels'. The comma is a decimal separator.

comparison_steps.description: Search for explicit origin field in invoice.
comparison_steps.details: Invoice contains explicit field 'Country of Origin: PORTUGAL'.
Certificate: 'PORTUGAL'. Invoice: 'PORTUGAL'. Exact match, no normalization needed.

rules_applied.description: Origin identified in explicit 'Country of Origin' field in invoice.
Positive verification – origin 'PORTUGAL' explicitly identified in invoice.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3: 'PORTUGAL'. Invoice field 'Country of Origin: PORTUGAL'. Exact textual match. No normalization required. Additionally, 'Port of Loading: LEIXOES, PORTUGAL' is consistent with Portuguese origin (though this is supplementary, not used as primary verification). MATCH per 4.5.5.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The certificate states 'EUROPEAN UNION' in the header — could this conflict with PORTUGAL as origin?
rejected_hypotheses.reason: Rejected. 'EUROPEAN UNION' in the certificate header identifies the certificate TYPE (EU Certificate of Origin), not the country of origin. Box 3 explicitly states 'PORTUGAL' as the country of origin. Portugal is an EU member state. No conflict exists.

rule_evaluation_log.details: No LC reference found in certificate. Certificate number SEG-26D-465117 does not constitute an LC reference.

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: Multi-document PDF handling and document pairing
rule_evaluation_log.details: Document A identified as Certificate of Origin. Document B identified as Commercial Invoice. Each file contains one document. Standard pairing applied.

Three criteria met: (1) Consignor J. UNGERTH AB found in invoice issuer, (2) Consignee MINA PACK found in invoice buyer, (3) Order number 25090 in certificate matches invoice Order No 25090. Pairing valid.

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: Koncernstruktur – search for consignor name in all invoice files
rule_evaluation_log.details: Certificate consignor 'J. UNGERTH AB' found as invoice issuer 'J.UNGERTH AB' in header, footer and VAT block. Space normalization applied. Same legal party confirmed.

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: Priority order for consignee identification – Buyer field is primary
rule_evaluation_log.details: Invoice has 'Buyer:' field (primary billing field) containing 'MINA PACK'. Certificate consignee is 'MINA PACK'. Exact match.

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: Priority 2 – Controlled text matching for goods description
rule_evaluation_log.details: Identity-bearing main designation 'KRAFTLINER' found exactly in invoice.

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: Occurrence check – numeric value 77.102 in invoice
rule_evaluation_log.details: Certificate: 77.102 MT net. Invoice: 77,102 in Quantity (MT) column. Identical numeric value.
Certificate states 'net'. Invoice states one quantity value without explicit GW/NW. Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2: single value, no contradicting alternative → MATCH.
Invoice explicit field 'Country of Origin: PORTUGAL' matches certificate box 3 'PORTUGAL'.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Alla kontrollpunkter stämmer överens. Avsändaren J. Ungerth AB, mottagaren Mina Pack, varubeskrivningen Kraftliner, kvantiteten 77,102 MT netto och ursprungslandet Portugal överensstämmer mellan certifikatet och fakturan. Dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.summary_of_findings: All five checkpoints verified as MATCH. Consignor J. UNGERTH AB matches invoice issuer (minor space normalization). Consignee MINA PACK matches invoice buyer exactly. Goods description KRAFTLINER is identical. Quantity 77.102 MT net matches. Country of origin PORTUGAL matches. No discrepancies found.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.certificate_value: J. UNGERTH AB, LILLA BOMMEN 2, SE-411 04, GÖTEBORG, SWEDEN
sections.invoice_value: J.UNGERTH AB, Lilla Bommen 2, SE-411 04 Gothenburg, SWEDEN
sections.rule_applied: Consignor name identified in invoice issuer block via direct identification (4.1.0). Space between 'J.' and 'UNGERTH' normalized. Country SWEDEN matches.
sections.motivation: Certificate consignor 'J. UNGERTH AB' was found as 'J.UNGERTH AB' in the invoice header (issuer), footer signature block, and via VAT number SE556146063401 (SE prefix = Sweden). The only difference is a single space between 'J.' and 'UNGERTH', which is an allowed normalization (extra mellanslag). Address: Lilla Bommen 2, SE-411 04 in both documents (Göteborg/Gothenburg is city name in Swedish/English — address differences do not cause MISMATCH per 4.1.5). Country SWEDEN is explicitly stated in both.

sections.certificate_value: MINA PACK, Behind El Tawheed & El Nour Plot 11, Block 12010 1st Industrial Zone, Northern Extension OBOUR CITY, EGYPT VAT 274401436
sections.invoice_value: MINA PACK, Behind El Tawheed & El Nour Plot 11, Block 12010 1st Industrial Zone, Northern Extension OBOUR CITY, EGYPT VAT 274401436
sections.rule_applied: Consignee identified in invoice primary billing field (Buyer). Exact name and address match.
sections.motivation: Certificate consignee 'MINA PACK' with full address matches exactly with invoice buyer 'MINA PACK' with identical address. Country EGYPT confirmed in both. VAT 274401436 also present in both. No normalization needed. Exact match.

sections.invoice_value: Description of Goods: KRAFTLINER / Quality/Grade: KRAFTLINER / Shipping Mark: ACID NUMBER: 2744014361001210016
sections.rule_applied: Priority 2 – controlled text matching. Identity-bearing main designation 'KRAFTLINER' found exactly in invoice.
sections.motivation: The identity-bearing main designation in the certificate is 'KRAFTLINER'. This exact term is found in the invoice's 'Description of Goods' and 'Quality/Grade' fields. Additional elements in the certificate (order number 25090, shipping mark ACID NUMBER 2744014361001210016, 59 reels) are all non-identity-bearing per 4.3.4 and are also confirmed in the invoice (Order No: 25090, Shipping Mark: ACID NUMBER: 2744014361001210016, Reels: 59). No article numbers present requiring Priority 1 verification.

sections.rule_applied: Main rule – occurrence check. Box 7 value verified against invoice quantity.
sections.motivation: Certificate box 7 states '77,102mt net' — interpreted as 77.102 metric tons, net weight (comma is decimal separator). Invoice states '77,102' in the 'Quantity (MT)' column — same numeric value 77.102 MT. Values are identical. Per 4.4.2.2 point 2: certificate states one weight value with 'net' category, and the invoice contains this same value without a contradicting alternative weight → MATCH. Additionally, 59 reels is confirmed in the invoice (Reels: 59), providing secondary corroboration.

sections.rule_applied: Direct verification – origin explicitly stated in invoice Country of Origin field.
sections.motivation: Certificate box 3 states 'PORTUGAL'. Invoice has explicit field 'Country of Origin: PORTUGAL'. Exact textual match. Additionally, Port of Loading is 'LEIXOES, PORTUGAL', consistent with Portuguese origin. Single origin country — no mixed origins complexity.

output_quality_checks.consistency_notes: All 5 checkpoints are MATCH with confidence ≥ 0.85. Overall result IDENTICAL is consistent with all checkpoints being MATCH. Workflow recommendation AUTO_APPROVAL_ELIGIBLE is consistent with IDENTICAL result. No MISMATCH or MANUAL_REVIEW on any checkpoint. Confidence floor (0.85) met for all checkpoints.
